months. Difficulties are mild overall, with noticeable impact in select contexts. Core features include slow
progress despite effort, reading difficulties, math difficulties, avoidance of homework, and irritability. A
safety screen notes a potential red-flag item that warrants clinical attention.  Cognitive screening indicates
a heterogeneous profile, with relative strength in visuospatial and relative weakness in processing speed
(other domains fall in the average range).   Key information is currently missing (e.g., cross-setting
symptoms (home vs school), developmental history, and learning assessment (reading/writing/math)), which
increases diagnostic uncertainty.
questions_to_ask_en: ['Any relevant perinatal/medical history?', 'What differences do caregivers and teachers report?', 'Have reading, writing, and math skills been formally assessed (psychoeducational testing)?', 'What was the response to targeted school interventions?', 'Are the symptoms present across settings (home, school, community), or situation-specific?']
should_defer_rationale_en: Defer: a red-flag item requires clinician-led risk assessment.
